title: Trouble Diagnosis — Preliminary Check (Cont'd) — Self Diagnosis

figures_detected.description: N-CVT Warning Lamp operation timing diagrams — showing OK (normal) and Fault (blinking) lamp behaviour relative to IGN-SW ON and Engine start events over time

content_blocks.text: The N-CVT warning lamp normally lights when the electrical system malfunctions and a problem is detected. The N-CVT warning lamp blinks if the electromagnetic clutch coil temperature rises excessively, or the motor stalls for a long period of time. The lamp goes off if the temperature lowers or the engine stall stops.
Refer to "Quick and Accurate Trouble Diagnosis" when the lamp blinks frequently.

In models equipped with ABS, turn ignition switch "ON", turn on power to CONSULT and touch "START". When the engine runs, the N-CVT warning lamp lights and self-diagnosis displayed indicates that the ABS operation signal path circuit is damaged. This is not the case, it is for test purpose only.

nodes.text: Check other troubles. Refer to "Diagnostic Procedures"
Perform test drive: Drive at full acceleration up to 50 km/h, then constant speed for 5 seconds. Decelerate to 30 km/h without pressing the brake pedal

content_blocks.text: Note 1: The N-CVT warning lamp lights when there is trouble in the electrical system. After the ignition switch is turned "OFF" and engine is started again, N-CVT warning lamp goes off.
Note 2: Check problem that occurred during driving. Record diagnosis results.
Note 4: Check problem that occurred during driving test. Record inspection results.

model_notes: ABS-equipped models: N-CVT warning lamp behaviour during CONSULT test is for test purpose only and does not indicate actual ABS signal path damage.
